daily_brief: The day's news is dominated by a rapidly expanding Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo, where WHO officials warned that vaccine development could take nine months and confirmed cases continue to rise, with at least 600 suspected infections and 139 deaths [BBC, Guardian, France 24]. US Secretary of State Rubio criticized the WHO's response while the US continues sweeping public health cuts following its withdrawal from the organization [Guardian]. In Beijing, Vladimir Putin concluded talks with Xi Jinping without securing the long-sought Power of Siberia 2 pipeline deal, though both leaders emphasized their strategic partnership and criticized US nuclear policy [BBC, Le Monde, Globe and Mail]. International condemnation mounted against Israeli National Security Minister Itamar Ben-Gvir after he posted video taunting handcuffed Gaza flotilla activists, drawing rare criticism from PM Netanyahu and prompting several nations to summon Israeli ambassadors [BBC, RFI, RNZ, France 24]. The Trump administration escalated pressure on Cuba by charging former president Raúl Castro with murder over the 1996 downing of two planes [BBC, Globe and Mail]. Meanwhile, German Chancellor Merz proposed a new 'associate member' status for Ukraine in the EU, offering representation without voting rights as a bridge toward full membership [France 24].

top_stories.headline: Ebola Outbreak in DRC Grows Rapidly as WHO Warns Vaccine Could Take Nine Months
bullets: WHO reports 600 suspected Ebola cases and 139 suspected deaths in DRC, with 51 confirmed cases in Ituri and North Kivu provinces and 2 confirmed in Uganda

Two candidate vaccines against the Bundibugyo strain are in development but have not completed clinical trials; WHO declared a public health emergency of international concern but assessed global risk as low
An American missionary doctor who contracted Ebola was evacuated to Germany along with his wife and four children for treatment and monitoring
US Secretary of State Rubio criticized WHO for being 'a little late' in identifying the outbreak, while the US has committed $13 million in assistance following its withdrawal from WHO last year

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Focused on WHO timeline and vaccine development challenges

sources.name: The Guardian
sources.framing_note: Emphasized US public health cuts and criticism of Rubio's comments; highlighted weak health infrastructure

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Framed around global health defunding and pandemic preparedness concerns

top_stories.headline: Putin Visits Beijing, Deepens Ties with Xi but Leaves Without Pipeline Deal
bullets: Putin and Xi met in Beijing, describing their partnership as at an 'unprecedented level' and jointly criticized US nuclear policy and Trump's Golden Dome missile defense plan
The Power of Siberia 2 natural gas pipeline deal, long sought by Russia, was not finalized during the visit
Western sanctions have pushed Russia further into economic dependence on China; analysts note Putin negotiates from relative weakness
The visit followed Trump's China trip last week, with Russian state media contrasting imagery of Putin-Xi closeness against Trump's departure

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Analysis emphasized geopolitical theater versus substantive outcomes; noted Putin's weaker negotiating position

sources.name: Le Monde
sources.framing_note: Headline emphasized Xi keeping Putin waiting on pipeline

sources.name: Globe and Mail
sources.framing_note: Highlighted how Trump's return has scrambled geopolitics and pushed countries toward China

top_stories.headline: International Outcry Over Israeli Minister's Video Taunting Detained Gaza Flotilla Activists
bullets: Israeli forces intercepted a 50-boat Gaza-bound aid flotilla off Cyprus; 430 activists from over 40 countries were detained and transferred to Israeli vessels

National Security Minister Itamar Ben-Gvir posted video showing himself taunting activists kneeling with hands bound, drawing condemnation from the US, UK, France, Italy, Canada, Australia, and New Zealand

PM Netanyahu issued rare criticism saying Ben-Gvir's actions were 'not in line with Israel's values'; rights group Adalah documented injuries including suspected broken ribs and alleged sexual harassment
Multiple countries summoned Israeli ambassadors; New Zealand had previously banned Ben-Gvir from travel for undermining peace prospects

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Balanced reporting on international reactions and rights group allegations

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Focused on French diplomatic response and video details

sources.name: RNZ
sources.framing_note: Highlighted New Zealand's prior travel ban on Ben-Gvir and strong government response

top_stories.headline: US Charges Former Cuban President Raúl Castro with Murder Over 1996 Plane Shootdown
bullets: The US indicted Raúl Castro, 94, on charges including conspiracy to kill US nationals, four counts of murder, and destruction of aircraft over the 1996 downing of two Brothers to the Rescue planes that killed four people
Castro and five Cuban military pilots were charged; murder counts carry maximum penalties of death or life imprisonment
Cuban President Díaz-Canel called the charges 'a political manoeuvre devoid of any legal foundation'
The indictment represents a major escalation in US pressure on Cuba's communist government as Trump pushes for regime change

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Straightforward reporting on charges and Cuban response

sources.name: Globe and Mail
sources.framing_note: Contextualized within broader Trump administration pressure campaign and hemispheric influence efforts

top_stories.headline: Germany Proposes 'Associate Member' Status for Ukraine in EU Without Voting Rights
bullets: Chancellor Friedrich Merz proposed granting Ukraine 'associate member' status allowing attendance at EU summits, representation in the Commission and Parliament, but without voting rights
The EU's mutual defense clause would apply to Ukraine under the proposal, and Kyiv could access parts of the EU budget
Merz acknowledged full accession cannot be completed soon due to 'countless hurdles' and ratification complexities
The proposal was outlined in a letter to EU Commission President von der Leyen and European Council President Costa

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Neutral reporting on proposal details and rationale

sources.name: Financial Times
sources.framing_note: Article behind paywall; headline indicates coverage of Germany's enlargement push

additional_stories.headline: US increases white South African refugee admissions to 17,500 citing 'emergency refugee situation'
Austrian former intelligence officer Egisto Ott convicted of spying for Russia and Wirecard fugitive Jan Marsalek
Sierra Leone receives nine deportees from US under agreement to accept up to 300 annually
Lithuania's president and prime minister forced into shelters during drone alert over Vilnius
UN General Assembly passes resolution backing ICJ climate change ruling with 141 votes in favor; US votes against
SpaceX files IPO prospectus in what is expected to be the most spectacular public offering ever
South Korea's KOSPI jumps 8.4% after Samsung union suspends planned strike
Paris appeals court to rule on Air France and Airbus appeal over 2009 Rio-Paris crash that killed 228
Cluster bombs used for first time in Mali conflict by army and Russian allies
Chinese FDI in Europe reaches seven-year high at €16.8 billion in 2025, led by Hungary
